metadata.title:
COATES AND CHANDLER
URGE new CHARTER

pages.raw_text:
d. c. Coates and George Chand-
Ter addressed a fair sized crowd
of men and women last night at
the Longfellow school in behalf of
the proposed new city charter.


Chandler denounced Spokanes
rotten government" ripped up
the ward heelers" and the ward
system, which the people of this
city relegated to the rear in the
amendments to the city charter
adopted two years ago. Mr.. Chand
Ter said the charter committee of
ID had considered all of the kicks
now being made against the new
charter and rejected them, and
that the charter committee there
fore had inserted all provisions
now contained in the new charter
with their eyes open.


Mr.. Coates was no so positive
as to the PROJECTIONS of the new
charter; in fact, he admitted that
it was full of imperfections and
that he had tried to cure most of
them, but had lost out: but he
urged the adoption of the charter
on the ground that it was far SUI
perior to the present one. Mr..
Coates said the only opposition to
the charter was coming from the
vicious element" the corpora
tons, the city hall ring" the con-
tractors ring and the saloon keep
ers. He extolled the preferential
system of voting as the panacea
Tor many of the ills that the city
hall has been heir to in the past.


one of the citizens present
brought up the experience of the
